bias_escape_phases.note: Telem bias_gz slope and audit Σdx_bias_gz by phase — escape colocated with COMPOSITION_BREAK, not with early SAME_DIR.

early_0.39_1.5.reading: No latch-specific bias escape. Latch innov smaller; Σdx_bias similar/milder.

late_1.5_2.0.reading: Bias escape (sign flip vs ctrl) colocated with innov COMPOSITION_BREAK.

reading.overall: NOT_TAUTOLOGICAL_UNIFORM_SCALE: early same-dir but not uniformly bigger; mean x2 dominated by late composition break (iz sign / cos collapse). Bias escape is late-colocated with the break — not early same-residual absorption.

verdict.label: COMPOSITION_BREAK_DRIVES_BIAS_ESCAPE
verdict.not_tautology: The aggregate innov ×2 (0.11→0.26) is NOT a uniform scale-up of the same residual. Early post-latch [0.39,1.5]: SAME direction, latch innov SMALLER — and no bias escape. Late [1.5,2.0]: direction BREAKS (median cos≈−0.65), latch innov ≫ ctrl, and nearly all latch bias_gz escape and Σ‖y‖ live here. Second reading wins for the interesting window.
verdict.implication_for_Z_family: Does NOT reduce cleanly to “close Z → yaw residue → nearest channel absorbs”. Early after latch that tautology would predict larger same-dir innov; we see the opposite. Closing Z remains the wrong sole lever (family closed). Escape into bias is mediated by a late composition change — K_bias att/vel decompose is justified, scoped to [1.5,2.0]s.
do_not: Treat aggregate innov×2 as sufficient mechanism without phase split
Skip K_bias decompose on tautology grounds — the late break argues for it
verdict.next: Decompose K_bias into att vs vel pathways, emphasis on ticks in [1.5,2.0]s where composition breaks.
